Details: Removal of tebuconazole from water by hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) was found to be very effective in one study (100 percent removal) [2145] when using photo-Fenton treatment.  A Fenton process is an advanced oxidation process which employs Fe2+ and H2O2.  Fenton reagents act as a photocatalyst under natural light irradiation process. Molecular oxygen, pH, and temperature are important parameters of the photo-Fenton process.
